axis: axis 105 Pilot-T1 — Llama-3.2-3B forward via TRIBE v2 multimodal

protocol.design: frozen H0 — 4-family prompts (Law/Phi/SelfRef/Hexad) × 16 _en per family, pairwise vertex Pearson r
protocol.PASS_criteria: inter-family vertex r < 0.7 in >= 1 pair AND intra-family vertex r > 0.85 for all 4 families
protocol.FAIL_criteria: pairwise vertex r > 0.95 across all 6 family pairs (axis collapse)
protocol.INCONCLUSIVE_criteria: numerically valid but neither PASS nor FAIL bounds
protocol.actual_status: no measurement obtained — pod never reached SSH-ready, model never loaded, no inference run

per_family_inter_intra_r_summary_reason: no inference performed — model never loaded; no maps generated; no Pearson r computable. Reporting null per raw#10 (no fabrication).

launch + poll wrapper scripts written and bash-syntax-checked

own_4_self_audit.step_1_diagnosis: Pre-SSH orchestrator stuck for full 15min watchdog window. Same fault signature as prior blocker pod 6chik4vxo5bkjv. Concurrent peer pod dqviqkw1jp5yej showed identical signature during same window — fault is platform-window correlated, not single-pod bad luck. ROOT CAUSE: RunPod cluster orchestrator pre-SSH stage degraded across 2026-04-27/28 dispatch window.
own_4_self_audit.step_2_canonical_fix: (a) DO NOT respawn alt-2 within same window — same root cause = same outcome. (b) Add canary-pod probe ($0.05) to detect orchestrator window status before any large dispatch. (c) Prefer fold-into-known-good-pod path (alt-3) once a canary pod confirms orchestrator window has cleared.
own_4_self_audit.step_3_harden: Watchdog protocol validated — limited cost from $3.40 (prior unbounded) to $0.765 (15min hard cap). Codify watchdog into orchestrator template. Pattern 8 in dispatch runbook = mandatory pre-SSH stuck-pod detection.
own_4_self_audit.step_4_evolve: Open RunPod support ticket with both pod IDs + timing evidence. Add canary-pod probe to anima_runpod_orchestrator.hexa as standard pre-flight gate for any pod > $1.50 budget. Update runbook docs/runpod_large_model_dispatch_template_20260427.md with Pattern 8 reference.

raw_10_caveats: No inference data exists for this run — verdict is DEFERRED, not PASS/FAIL/INCONCLUSIVE. Reporting null Pearson r summary honestly.
Cost $0.765 was burned for zero measurement. This is RunPod billing-as-allocated semantics; no user error.
The reproduction across two pods (6chik4vxo5bkjv + sowpcnrlsbd0k7) plus concurrent peer (dqviqkw1jp5yej) is strong but not proof of platform-wide outage — sample size 3 within ~17h window.
No on-pod evidence (no install.log, no preflight.log, no wrapper.log) was obtainable because SSH never came up. The dispatch wrapper /tmp/anima_pilot_t1_alt1_launch.sh was never executed.
TRIBE v2 dependency stack was NOT verified on this pod — that risk remains untouched and untested for the next attempt.

Alt-2 (batch dispatch) was not tried — would likely have the same fault if the platform-window hypothesis holds. Alt-3 (fold into existing pod) is currently blocked because the candidate fold-target pod (axis 96, dqviqkw1jp5yej) is also stuck.
HF token gating for meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B was previously verified HTTP 200 and remains valid — not a contributing factor.
raw#37 transient .py wrappers were correctly used; persistent state lives at state/v11_pilot_t1_llama_3_2_3b_resume/ per mandate.
next_cycle_recommendation: STEP 1 (canary, $0.05, 5 min): spawn a single tiny pod (1× cheapest GPU, 20GB disk) and time-to-SSH-ready as orchestrator-window probe. If < 5min: window has cleared, proceed. If > 5min: defer 60-90 min and retry probe.

STEP 2 (preferred fold-into, $0.30, 15 min): once axis 96 step1 pod brings up cleanly OR a fresh axis 105 pod brings up cleanly, fold the Pilot-T1 inference into that single pod's wrapper rather than spawning a dedicated alt-2 pod — one SSH surface, one orchestrator-stuck-risk surface.

STEP 3 (escalation): if 2 canaries in a row stay stuck > 5min, register this as an active RunPod platform incident, open a support ticket with pod IDs 6chik4vxo5bkjv + sowpcnrlsbd0k7, and pause Pilot-T1 dispatch until incident resolved.
